task_completion.description: Verify that Developer Options have been enabled on the device.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Developer options' menu item in the Settings app, or a toast message stating 'You are now a developer!'.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the 'Running services' screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the 'Running services' screen within the Developer Options menu, displaying device memory status and active apps.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent located the 'Flight Crew View' application within the Running services list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing 'Flight Crew View' listed among the active services on the Running services screen, with its current RAM usage visible.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent saved the memory value to /sdcard/ram_audit.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal command (e.g., echo 'XX MB' > /sdcard/ram_audit.txt) or a text editor saving the RAM usage value to the exact path /sdcard/ram_audit.txt.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent captured a screenshot of the Running services screen showing the application's entry to /sdcard/ram_evidence.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a terminal command (e.g., screencap -p /sdcard/ram_evidence.png) executed while the Running services screen displaying the 'Flight Crew View' app was active.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Android Settings GUI to navigate to Running services rather than just querying memory via shell commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate screenshots showing the Settings app, 'About emulated device' (tapping build number), and 'Developer options' menus.

integrity.description: Verify the memory value saved to the audit file matches the actual value displayed in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The RAM value written to /sdcard/ram_audit.txt matches the exact RAM usage number displayed next to 'Flight Crew View' in the Running services GUI screenshot.

integrity.description: Verify the evidence screenshot was genuinely captured from the active screen and not faked.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screencap command or screenshot action is performed immediately after or while the GUI shows the Running services screen with the target app visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The audit file must be saved exactly to '/sdcard/ram_audit.txt'. The evidence screenshot must be saved exactly to '/sdcard/ram_evidence.png'. The target application to look for in Running services is 'Flight Crew View'.
